items.rationale: He calls stablecoins the 'killer app' with clear product-market fit; sub-penny fees make crypto payment rails a no-brainer that can bring billions onchain and bootstrap a global payment network.

citations.quote: Stablecoins are the "killer app" of crypto, and here's a bunch of great data that shows it.
Stablecoin payments and AI agents are some of the clearest use cases for crypto since Bitcoin's store of value.
Adjusted Stablecoin Transaction Volume... Stablecoin adoption is on the rise as infra developments bring down fees.

items.rationale: Only 5-10% of ~617M owners transact; he frames closing this gap as a bigger opportunity than growing total investors, and named it his core 2025 thesis.

citations.quote: Let's close the gap between crypto owners and active on-chain users. This is more important... than expanding the total number of crypto investors.
I believe this is the year to re-engage the 617M "passive" crypto holders and bring the world onchain.

items.rationale: Blockchains are a counterbalance to centralized AI, enabling permissionless compute/data markets; and AI agents as primary crypto users could erase traditional UX barriers.

citations.quote: Decentralized blockchains are a counterbalancing force to centralized AI.
Crazy to think that if AI agents become the primary users of crypto, a lot of the UX challenges... go away.

items.rationale: His favorite adoption indicator; repeated all-time highs (35M monthly users by Dec 2024) signal UX improvements drawing mainstream global users, with 20x+ headroom.

citations.quote: The number of monthly users hit an all-time high of 35 million in December.
Another month, another all time high for mobile wallet users.

items.rationale: An 'underrated' category he became convinced is critical to onboarding a billion people, because it imports contributors with incentives exogenous to the protocol (e.g., ESPN resolving markets).
items.age_note: Emphasized heavily July-Aug 2024; not restated since, so treated as persisted.

citations.quote: zkTLS / zkEmail is an underrated category in crypto.
I'm now convinced that web proofs/zkTLS will play a critical role in getting us there.

items.rationale: Expects a >1,000x explosion in AI-generated content that legacy distribution/IP rails can't handle; blockchains can provide the open global coordination, provenance, and curation layer.

citations.quote: We need an open, global coordination layer in order for AI generated content to thrive. And this is exactly what blockchains can provide.
We desperately need an open, global coordination layer for IP, and I couldn't be more excited about what Story is building!

items.rationale: Sees them as a powerful real-time truth-surfacing mechanism whose accuracy improves with broader (esp. US) participation; cites the post-legalization odds shift as evidence.

citations.quote: Prediction markets have proven to be a powerful tool for surfacing real time information.
the odds notably shifted right after U.S. people... were allowed to legally participate.

items.rationale: Open protocols are producing vibrant developer ecosystems; he speculates on emergent data/AI-training opportunities built atop Farcaster's open data.

citations.quote: The Decentralized Social experiment is underway, and vibrant developer ecosystems are emerging around open protocols like Farcaster.
is there a future where Farcaster data is used to train AI models?... Maybe there's even a way to build a sustainable, open protocol around all this.

items.rationale: Views DAO treasuries as an underappreciated funding pool larger than annual crypto VC, and is encouraged by expanding legal frameworks for them.

citations.quote: DAOs are an underappreciated source of funding for builders.
Huge news for expanding the ways in which DAOs can legally operate.

items.rationale: Cited as a promising early experiment for unlocking idle physical resources (GPUs, storage, telecom) and democratizing access.

citations.quote: we're also seeing promising early experiments in areas like gaming, social, and DePIN.

items.rationale: Acknowledges it's early days but is enthusiastic about using blockchains for hard scientific problems and explicitly wants more builders to enter.

citations.quote: It's early days for DeSci, but I'm grateful for entrepreneurs... using blockchains to solve some of the hardest... problems in the world!
would love to see more builders enter this space!

items.rationale: Repeatedly frames offshoring of web3 development as a national-security risk akin to semiconductors. Note: by early 2025 his tone shifted toward optimism as US regulatory/political tailwinds strengthened.
items.age_note: Concern strongest 2023-2024; by Oct 2024-Feb 2025 he sounds more optimistic on US policy ('political tailwinds have been strong'; praises CFTC leadership), so the worry has partly resolved.

citations.quote: there are signs that the US may be losing its lead in web3... This is a matter of national security.
Keeping crypto onshore is a matter of national security.

items.rationale: Worries that AI development is concentrated among a handful of tech giants operating black boxes; sees crypto as possibly the only credible counterbalancing force.

citations.quote: This consolidation is worrisome. Crypto might be the only credible counterbalancing force out there.
Black box algorithms controlled by a few large companies won't cut it.

items.rationale: Abundant AI content and deepfakes threaten information integrity; needs proof-of-authenticity protocols plus decentralized incentivized curation to surface good content.

citations.quote: it also creates a world of abundant AI-generated content, including deep fakes.
authentication / provenance is important, but so is curation so that we can surface the good AI content.

items.rationale: Low fees and incentives have made raw active-address counts a poor proxy for real users, complicating the industry's ability to gauge genuine adoption.

citations.quote: Active addresses have lately become a really bad measure for users (low fees + incentives to game).

items.rationale: Raised the risk that embedding wallets too deeply into apps could misalign with the self-custody/ownership vision of web3. Posed as a debate rather than a firm stance.
items.age_note: Stated once (July 2024) as an open question and never revisited.

citations.quote: There is a real risk of over-abstracting wallets and blockchains from users.

items.rationale: Doubling of wholesale CBDC development post-sanctions threatens US dollar dominance; argues USD stablecoins are a better answer than a sovereign CBDC.

citations.quote: wholesale CBDC developments have DOUBLED. This is a national security concern.

items.rationale: His clearest conviction asset class — sub-penny L2 USDC transfers achieved product-market fit and are the on-ramp for mass adoption.

citations.quote: USDC transfers on Ethereum L2s now cost less than a penny.
Over 99% are denominated in USD... why not embrace what's already working?

items.rationale: Repeatedly cited as the top chain by monthly active addresses and a leader in low-fee DEX volume — framed as observational evidence of low-fee adoption rather than a price call.

citations.quote: low-transaction fee blockchains like @base and @solana captured a majority of this activity.
The top chain by monthly active addresses: @solana.

items.rationale: Highlighted alongside Solana as a low-fee chain capturing the majority of DEX activity, consistent with his thesis that cheap blockspace drives real usage.

citations.quote: low-transaction fee blockchains like @base and @solana captured a majority of this activity.

items.rationale: Names ETP net flows for BTC and ETH as a key 2025 metric to watch, expecting institutional exposure to translate into rising flows — a structural-adoption read, not a trade.

citations.quote: As more institutional investors seek to get exposure to crypto assets, it should translate into increased net flows for the ETPs.

items.rationale: Dismisses them as the next evolution of the gambling industry rather than a core value driver — consistent with his repeated insistence that speculation is a byproduct, not the point.

citations.quote: Meme coins are just the next evolution of the gambling industry... gen z is in the trenches.
speculation is a byproduct that is separate from its core value proposition.

risk_regime.rationale: Consistently and increasingly bullish, builder-oriented optimist. Entering 2025 he calls it 'another exciting year' / 'looking bright', points to adoption all-time highs, sub-penny fees, strengthening US political tailwinds, and the price-innovation cycle. He is structurally long crypto's fundamentals while explicitly separating speculation from core value — so his risk-on posture is about technology adoption, not chasing price.

notes: Daren is a data/metrics-driven a16z crypto team member (author of the State of Crypto report); his 'views' skew toward adoption fundamentals and infrastructure rather than directional token bets, so token entries are weaker-conviction observations than his sector theses. Most recent (Q4 2024-Q1 2025) emphasis: stablecoins, re-engaging passive holders, AI agents/crypto-x-AI, and mobile-wallet adoption. The clearest evolution over the corpus is his US-policy concern softening into optimism by early 2025 as regulatory tailwinds improved (captured in age_note). zkTLS and the wallet-over-abstraction worry are 2024 stances not restated by Q1 2025, so marked persisted. He repeatedly disclaims that nothing he posts is investment advice.
